# Example: a constellation

The constellation is the part that's hard to picture from prose, so here's a worked example —
**generated by `briefing.py`, not hand-drawn.**

## The scenario

An umbrella project, `acme-platform`, made of three components, each with its own briefing:

- `acme-api` — the backend, open-source (MIT), shipped
- `acme-web` — the web app, commercial, mid-build
- `acme-mobile` — the mobile app, closed, still a prototype

## What you write (in each child briefing)

Each child declares its parent in the header meta line. `briefing.py new <child> --parent
acme-platform --layer <tag>` stamps it; you keep `status` current as a one-line headline:

```
<!-- meta: parent=acme-platform | layer=OPEN-MIT    | projectdir=~/code/acme-api    | status=shipped; v1.3 on npm; stable -->
<!-- meta: parent=acme-platform | layer=COMMERCIAL  | projectdir=~/code/acme-web    | status=in build; checkout flow WIP -->
<!-- meta: parent=acme-platform | layer=CLOSED      | projectdir=~/code/acme-mobile | status=prototype; blocked on API auth -->
```

To bubble a blocker or decision up to the parent, add a clean `[surface]` line in the child's
§4/§5/§6 (prefix form, never inside a table cell). For example, in `acme-mobile`'s §4:

```
[surface] blocked on shared auth tokens from acme-api (see api #214)
```

## What `rollup` produces (in the parent briefing's §2)

Run `briefing.py rollup acme-platform` after bumping any child. It scans every briefing that
declares `parent=acme-platform` and regenerates this block — **derived, never hand-edited:**

<!-- constellation:start (managed by agent-session-briefing rollup — do not hand-edit) -->
**Components**

| Component | Version | Layer | Status | Updated | Briefing |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| acme-api | v1.3 | OPEN-MIT | shipped; v1.3 on npm; stable | 2026-06-02 | [↗](../acme-api/SESSION_BRIEFING.md) |
| acme-mobile | v1.1 | CLOSED | prototype; blocked on API auth | 2026-06-02 | [↗](../acme-mobile/SESSION_BRIEFING.md) |
| acme-web | v1.0 | COMMERCIAL | in build; checkout flow WIP | 2026-06-02 | [↗](../acme-web/SESSION_BRIEFING.md) |

**Surfaced from components** — blockers / decisions needing attention here
- **acme-mobile:** blocked on shared auth tokens from acme-api (see api #214)
- **acme-web:** needs the /checkout API finalized before launch — gating Q3
<!-- constellation:end -->

## What to notice

- **The matrix is derived.** Versions, layers, statuses, and dates come straight from each child's
  header — nobody maintains this table by hand, so it can't go stale. Bump a child, re-run
  `rollup`, and the parent is current.
- **`[surface]` is a spotlight, not a copy.** Only the lines you tag bubble up; the parent stays a
  glanceable index, not a dump of every child's open questions. Untag a line and it drops on the
  next rollup.
- **`layer` is whatever dimension matters to you.** Here it's the open-core seam
  (MIT / commercial / closed); it could just as easily be team ownership or maturity. Leave it
  `unset` if you don't need it.
- **You start with one.** This three-node constellation is just three single-project briefings that
  happen to share a parent. Add nodes as projects appear.
